retry_reason: The get only fails when dfa is NULL, meaning no valid object exists; retrying with the same NULL pointer will again result in NULL, so retry is pointless.
contract_summary: aa_get_dfa is an unconditional get-wrapper for struct aa_dfa objects. It increments the kref reference count if the dfa pointer is non-NULL and returns the same pointer. The caller obtains a new reference to the dfa object and must balance this call with a corresponding put (aa_put_dfa) when the reference is no longer needed. The return value must be checked: if NULL was passed in, NULL is returned, and no reference is acquired. This wrapper does not allocate or initialize the object; it assumes the object already exists with a valid kref.

judge.reason: Primary purpose is to increment the refcount on an aa_dfa object via kref_get and return the dfa pointer. Only logic outside the refcount operation is a NULL guard, which is standard for defensive get wrappers.
